title: bomarea andimarcana subsp. andimarcana
intro: Bomarea andimarcana subsp. andimarcana, also known as the Andean Glory of the Snow, is a species of flowering plant in the family Alstroemeriaceae. It is a type of flowering bulbous perennial native to the Andes mountains in South America, traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains alkaloids with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin problems. Herbal preparations of Bomarea andimarcana subsp. andimarcana include infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which are often used to treat a range of health issues, from colds and flu to skin irritations and wounds.
